fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer pharmacist license to illinois, pharmacist compact illinois, illinois pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Illinois Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
320 W. Washington Street, 3rd Floor
Springfield, IL 62786

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Illinois.

steps.title: Create an IDFPR Online Services Portal Account
steps.description: Register for a user account on the IDFPR Online Services Portal to access the online application system.

steps.title: Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Log in to the IDFPR Online Services Portal, select 'Apply for a New License', choose 'Pharmacist', and complete the application form. Pay the required application and initial licensure fees online.

steps.title: Provide Official Transcripts and Licensure Verification
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your pharmacy education program to be sent directly to IDFPR. Request verification of licensure from all states where you have held a pharmacist license; this is often facilitated through the NABP's Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (e-LTP).

steps.title: Pass the Illinois Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass the Illinois Multi-State Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). Information for scheduling this exam is typically found on the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) website.

steps.title: Submit Additional Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit any other required supporting documents as specified in the application instructions, such as proof of name change (if applicable), directly to IDFPR.

steps.title: Await Licensure Decision
steps.description: Monitor your application status through the IDFPR Online Services Portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Department.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Illinois pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Illinois
hero.dek: Users can apply for a Pharmacist license in Illinois, including by endorsement (reciprocity), through the IDFPR Online Services Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account: 1. Navigate to the IDFPR Online Services Portal. 2. Click on 'Register for an Account' to create a new user ID and password. 3. Follow the prompts to complete registration. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License' to begin your Pharmacist application.
